title: A nonnegative two-row row-stochastic operator is coordinatewise infinity-norm stable

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If both rows of a two-by-two operator are nonnegative and row-stochastic, then inputs bounded by M in absolute value produce two outputs bounded by M.
assumptions: all four row weights are nonnegative
informal.expected_conclusion: Both output coordinates are bounded in absolute value by M.

declarations.name: row2Apply
declarations.statement: def row2Apply (a b x y : Real) : Real := a * x + b * y

declarations.name: rowStochastic2First
declarations.statement: def rowStochastic2First (a b x y : Real) : Real := row2Apply a b x y

declarations.name: rowStochastic2Second
declarations.statement: def rowStochastic2Second (c d x y : Real) : Real := row2Apply c d x y

declarations.name: rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le (a b c d x y M : Real) (ha : 0 <= a) (hb : 0 <= b) (hc : 0 <= c) (hd : 0 <= d) (hrow1 : a + b = 1) (hrow2 : c + d = 1) (hx : |x| <= M) (hy : |y| <= M) : |rowStochastic2First a b x y| <= M /\ |rowStochastic2Second c d x y| <= M

tasks.input: Translate coordinatewise infinity-norm stability of a nonnegative two-row row-stochastic operator into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le (a b c d x y M : Real) (ha : 0 <= a) (hb : 0 <= b) (hc : 0 <= c) (hd : 0 <= d) (hrow1 : a + b = 1) (hrow2 : c + d = 1) (hx : |x| <= M) (hy : |y| <= M) : |rowStochastic2First a b x y| <= M /\ |rowStochastic2Second c d x y| <= M

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by applying row2Apply_abs_le to each row.
tasks.target: Split the conjunction and solve each row with row2Apply_abs_le.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma needed for each row-stability coordinate.
